messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "nl-grid-freeze-share-erosion",
 "title": "The Netherlands' 0.28 slice is legally and electrically frozen for the entire Year-1 window",
 "narrative": "geo.NL = 0.28 is one of the best-triangulated nodes in the ledger — Statista and the DDA agree within 3%. But both measure installed stock, and the venture sells into flow: new fit-outs where a new vendor can be designed in. In NL that flow is administratively shut for the whole first-12-months window: a national decree bans new hyperscale builds outside two municipalities, Amsterdam's draft zoning plan caps connections at 350 MVA until 2030, and distribution firms say no power is available until 2030 — with Amsterdam holding ~70% of national colo. What remains in NL is replacement demand inside operating halls, the most incumbent-locked business there is. Nearly a third of the funnel is a stock share masquerading as an addressable flow.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "geo.NL"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "National hyperscale ban outside Het Hogeland/Hollands Kroon, Amsterdam's 350 MVA connection ceiling to 2030, and grid operators reporting no power available until 2030",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-NL#c7",
    "digest:geo-NL#c8",
    "digest:geo-NL#c9"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Amsterdam Metro holds ~70% of Dutch colo capacity, so the caps bind the bulk of the country; NL growth already decelerated to 4.9% CAGR 2021–23 — the flow of new fit-outs where a new PDU vendor gets specified approaches zero in the Year-1 window",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-NL#c12",
    "digest:geo-NL#c13"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "NL's share of contestable Year-1 demand is far below its 0.28 installed-capacity share; the geography mix overweights a frozen market and inflates SAM and YAM proportionally",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:geo.NL"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The node is triangulated with two independent sources agreeing within ~3% — it looks like a solved cell. The failure mode is not measurement error but metric choice: installed MW is the wrong proxy for a new entrant's reachable demand in a market where new construction is banned and the grid is allocated to 2030.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Liander/Stedin announcing material new data-center grid allocations before 2030, or the DDA reporting >80 MW of newly commissioned Dutch colocation capacity within the venture's Year-1.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.55,
  "rationale": "The ban, the MVA cap and the grid unavailability statements are enacted or on the record for exactly this window; leakage exists via the Eemshaven/Agriport exception zones and private-substation workarounds like the Pure DC 78 MW campus, so the freeze is partial rather than total.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "geo.NL",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.2,
   "note": "Contestable NL slice shrinks in absolute terms (frozen new-build flow); deliberately not re-normalizing siblings — this is a smaller slice, not a mix shift"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Adoption status of Amsterdam's umbrella zoning plan and the 350 MVA connection ceiling",
   "where": "Gemeente Amsterdam planning docket and Dutch Data Center Association policy updates",
   "threshold": "Plan adopted with the 350 MVA cap intact",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "National colocation capacity print year-on-year",
   "where": "DDA 'State of the Dutch Data Centers' annual report",
   "threshold": "National colo MW flat at ≤950 MW",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Obtain Liander/Stedin/TenneT NL connection-queue data for the data-center category to quantify how much NL fit-out actually proceeds through 2027",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "geo.NL",
   "note": "Replaces the stock-based 0.28 with a flow-based reachable share"
  },
  {
   "action": "Concentrate NL go-to-market on the exception zones (Eemshaven, Agriport) and private-substation projects where new builds are still legal and powered",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "demand-discontinuity",
 "riskId": "risk.demand-discontinuity.nl-grid-freeze-share-erosion"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
